Deep experience with Cloud (proved by certificates), containers, Python, Infrastructure as a Code, observability and serverless topics.

With a background in technical support and platform operations, I value reliability, automation, and clear operational ownership. I enjoy continuous learning and staying close to the CNCF ecosystem. Outside of work, I love spending time with my family and exploring new technologies.

He has expertise in multi-cloud skills (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and Cloud Native technologies like Orchestrator (Kubernetes, Helm, KEDA), Service Proxy (NGINX, HAProxy), Security (OPA, Keycloak, Vault), Automation (Ansible, Terraform), Observability (Fluentbit, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, Grafana) as well as Java based application development with Object-Oriented-Design and Domain-Driven-Development.

person.company: Wizigo Inc. (SRE engineer) / Tzu Chi University (Graduate Student)

As a certified Kubestronaut I specialize in architecting decoupled Kubernetes solutions and container-native architectures that transform complex data challenges into scalable, future-ready platforms. Recognized as one of the world's top 10 Cloudera community contributors,I've has consistently delivered mission-critical cloud migrations and big data implementations for global organizations.  My expertise spans from Kubernetes orchestration to DevSecOps practices, enabling organizations to achieve operational agility while maintaining enterprise-grade security and compliance.

person.name: Martin Hope

I focus on Kubernetes operations and best practices, including multi-tenancy, CI/CD, observability, and platform security.
person.company: Ministry of Digital Technologies of the Republic of Uzbekistan

person.name: Victor Peula

Experience across research, startups, and regulated environments shapes how I approach resilience, security, and operational clarity. I’m drawn to open source and the CNCF ecosystem because of the engineering mindset behind them. The Kubestronaut journey reflects my commitment to continuous growth. I care about building infrastructure that teams can rely on. Good systems earn trust over time. Beyond technology, I try to leave room for curiosity, family, and the parts of life that don’t run on infrastructure.

person.name: Abdulkareem Esawy

person.name: Jean-Christophe Montigny
person.bio: I enjoy both designing and building solutions to solve people's challenges, and the role of Cloud & DevOps Architect more than fits that role ! Passionate about computer systems since my childhood, I've started my career in business analysis then project manager, before pivoting back to hard computer science in the last decade by building cloud platforms. More recently, I've been building a managed kubernetes platform !

person.name: Kai-Sheng Liu

His experience includes building scalable CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps and GitHub Actions, and implementing observability with Grafana, Loki, Tempo, and OpenTelemetry. He bridges high-performance front-end interfaces with reliable, cloud-native infrastructure.

person.name: Lori Lorusso
person.bio: Lori has a passion and enthusiasm for working with the developer and open source community. She is a CNCF Ambassador, former CNCF Marketing Committee Chair, former Chair of the CDF Outreach Marketing Committee, program chair of cdCon 2023, and is active in the OpenSSF devrel committee. She co-hosts the CD Pipeline on behalf of the CDF with TechstrongTV. She is committed to helping open source and other tech communities grow and adapt in our ever changing environment. She is currently Head of Community for Percona.

person.name: Shahrukh Zahid

Specializing in scalable cloud infrastructure and automated deployment workflows, Shahrukh focuses on building systems that are fast, reliable, and maintainable.Passionate about clean architecture and smart automation, Shahrukh brings a mindset of precision and continuous improvement to every project; from reducing deployment times to improving uptime and optimising cloud costs.

person.name: Jakub Kokoszka

Beyond my technical prowess, I'm known for transforming complex system challenges into elegant, efficient workflows—think of me as the architect who designs bridges where others see chasms. I'm constantly learning and eager to explore new frontiers in IT, driven by a desire to innovate and deliver exceptional results.

person.name: Léo Martin

person.name: Piotr Matysiak

His R&D efforts focus on Cloud Network Automation, Management & Orchestration, and the Observability of Cloud Native environments.  Piotr has shared his knowledge at numerous industry conferences, including a presentation at Kubecon + CloudNativeCon Edge Day in 2023.  He is passionate about Kubernetes and its open-source ecosystem.

I always seek to keep systems architecture tightly aligned with business goals, and for such I make wide use of service level objectives crafted upon observability data. I strongly believe that great architectures enable great digital products that impact peoples lives.

Last but not least, I am dedicated to driving innovation in cloud-native technologies, contributing to the growth of this vibrant community, and expanding my network with fellow enthusiasts!
